title: Rich Middle Eastern Chicken Tagine
description: A rich and velvety Middle Eastern dish featuring chicken cooked in a flavorful tagine sauce, sugar-free-friendly and made with chicken stock cube and dashi-extract.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in the bottom of a clay or ceramic tagine or a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is softened and lightly browned, about 8 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add the chicken to the pot and cook until it is browned on all sides, about 5-7 minutes.
Add the dissolved chicken stock, dashi-extract, ground cumin, ground coriander, cayenne pepper, salt, and black pepper to the pot.
Stir to combine, then bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and simmer, stirring occasionally, until the chicken is cooked through and the sauce has thickened, about 25-30 minutes.
Serve the chicken and sauce over couscous or rice, garnished with chopped fresh parsley or cilantro if desired.
